description: You are assisting a data-scientist who is cleaning several large CSV datasets and wants to share a temporary Jupyter Notebook with colleagues. The Notebook will listen on TCP port 9876. The scientist does not have root privileges on this server, so the real firewall can’t be changed directly. Instead, the team keeps a **plain-text manifest** of desired firewall rules in the user-writable directory /home/user/firewall_config. A nightly CI job (run with elevated privileges) parses that manifest and applies the rules system-wide. 1. Make sure the directory /home/user/firewall_config exists. 2. Inside that directory, create (or update) the file /home/user/firewall_config/rules.conf. 3. Append **exactly one** line that declares the desired rule for the upcoming Jupyter session: allow tcp 9876 # jupyter-cleaning (all lower-case, single spaces as shown, followed by a single newline). 4. After writing the rule, create a verification log file named /home/user/firewall_config/rules.log that contains **only** the complete, final contents of rules.conf. The log should be produced by reading the file, not by manually typing its expected text. The automated grader will inspect: • the presence of the directory /home/user/firewall_config • the presence and contents of /home/user/firewall_config/rules.conf • the presence and contents of /home/user/firewall_config/rules.log For both rules.conf and rules.log the grader expects **identical** text, byte-for-byte, consisting solely of: allow tcp 9876 # jupyter-cleaning⏎ (where “⏎” denotes a single trailing newline, and there is **no other text** before or after.) • Achieve the goal with a single, simple terminal command if possible. • Do not use sudo or attempt to modify the real system firewall. • The agent has write permission in /home/user but no elevated privileges. The task is complete when both files exist with the exact contents specified above, and the verification log accurately mirrors rules.conf.
truth:
<!-- Files/directories that should exist before the task starts -->
    <!-- (none) -->

    <!-- Expected final state -->
    <directory path="/home/user/firewall_config" should_exist="yes" />

    <file path="/home/user/firewall_config/rules.conf">
        allow tcp 9876 # jupyter-cleaning\n
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/firewall_config/rules.log">
        allow tcp 9876 # jupyter-cleaning\n
    </file>
